reviews.author: Daniel Beaudoin
reviews.text: One of the most beautiful locations I visited in Vietnam, the view of the mountain is incredible but more than that the grasslands, water buffalo, caves and waterfalls were amazing. If you want to see one of the most scenic places this is it.

reviews.author: Mariana
reviews.text: No water in January and the view is pretty simple. You can see a lot of similar views while doing the loop. So skippable, but nice if you are in the area. There is a small cafe right on the viewpoint - don’t hesitate to take coffee. The black one was awesome, creamy with a great aftertaste.

reviews.author: Leon Wüst
reviews.text: I liked it very much!!! It was definetely my favourite spot in Vietnam. Don't miss out on taking the hike to the cave on top. We were the only people, a cute dog followed us all the way and went exploring with us. The hike is maybe 20 mins from the bottom of the mountain. You will have to circle the mountain to the left side, cross the river on the small wooden bridge and use the path that is made of beton first, then it will change to a farmers field. I think there is other possible ways as well but this is the one we found.

reviews.author: Josh Lawrence
reviews.text: We visited during November, there was no lake. During this time we walked down towards the mountain and wandered both to the right and left (if facing God’s Eye Mountain). To the right was a river, to the left was a drying up lake with endless areas to explore. Further left were karst rock fields. Truly a beautiful area. We spent about 3 hours here.

reviews.author: Mikey P
reviews.text: This place is out of this world! My favourite in Cao bang and maybe Vietnam! The ride in on the bike is a bit bumpy since the last 5-10 mins is dirt but easy if dry weather. Recommend staying grabbing a drink on the hill and enjoying the view especially in the late evening, then walking down (road is very poor condition for bikes) and going much closer to the mountain, not too far of a walk and quite easy just follow the shoreline if it's wet season and full of water. Lots of buffalo grazing which is awesome also.

reviews.author: Sim Sim
reviews.text: This is the right pin on google maps to get here!!! There are some other pins but they lead you nowhere other then steep mountain road and no heavensgate/Gods eye (nice trip though😜) You will (at this moment 11-27-2025) be stopped by some roadwork, you can get passed with a scooter or by foot and drive a bit further though. We parked the scooter with a couple of kids for 10k/ €0,40 and walked the rest, about 10/15 minutes, ok. Then you’ll see Heavens gate! Everything great, nice view, but no lake at this moment. Don’t complain when the lake is gone, its nature, not Disneyworld. Just enjoy!

reviews.author: Ruth W
reviews.text: this is such an amazing natural phenomenon that's worth stopping by for a look. to see a hole in the middle of a tall formation due to water erosion is insane. i saw some boats taking tourists closer towards the mountain so that's an option too. definitely stop by this sight if you're in cao bang!

reviews.author: matthieu destandau
reviews.text: Visited when the water had gone. While the mountain is very pretty the plastic polution is sad to see... a nice ride to the site on a sunny day.

reviews.author: Tom Engelmann
reviews.text: Our personal highlight in Vietnam. Beautiful ride with a scooter from Cao Bang along the loop. The nature is stunning. Can recommend walking towards the mountain

reviews.author: Dream
reviews.text: This is an absolutely stunning place, the kind of rare and magical spot that feels untouched by time. Best of all, you can visit completely free of charge, making it even more special. The sweeping views are breathtaking in every direction, and the presence of the horses grazing peacefully adds an extra layer of charm and beauty to the landscape. It’s the perfect place to pause, take in the scenery, and let the tranquillity wash over you.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: God’s Eye Mountain is epic! If you’re anywhere near Cao Bằng, this spot is a must-see! God’s Eye Mountain absolutely lives up to its name — a dramatic karst peak with a perfectly sculpted hole right through the middle. It looks surreal, like something out of a fantasy film or nature documentary. Standing in front of it, the sheer size and shape of the arch makes it feel like the earth opened a portal through the stone. There’s a peaceful stream that winds through the valley below and the surrounding hills are lush and untouched, adding to the wild, remote vibe. If you’re into photography, this place is a dream — especially with the symmetrical reflection in the river and the towering mountains surrounding it. There’s a viewpoint trail that gives you a fantastic perspective of the mountain framed by the valley. The track can be a little uneven and rocky in places, so good footwear helps — but it’s manageable and totally worth it. This is one of those rare places that feels like you’ve discovered something secret. Far fewer tourists than more popular spots, but that’s part of what makes it so special. Definitely one of the highlights of northern Vietnam for me! Pity about the day wished I’d had some blue sky.

reviews.author: KenDano Lambert
reviews.text: Most incredible place in Vietnam. Okay, Vietnam is fuuull of jaw dropping scenery and awesome landscapes but let me show you... this place has it all. Serene valley nestled between stunning Mountains, full of wild horses, water buffalo and precious dogs. There are plentiful trails for hiking and even pools for bathing nearby the waterfall... yeah, there's even a WATERFALL! Who doesn't love all these things jam packed together in one walkable setting? Wait until you see this place.

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: Amazing landscape! I came in Nov few years ago. There is camping site, but no shops around, you need to prepare everything, bring food and drink by yourself.

reviews.author: Marc
reviews.text: Amazing place. Super easy to get to via scooter, but be careful that once you’re there, driving the last bit downhill can be a bit too steep/difficult. So park your scooter maybe. We’ve only been there for a short duration during sunset, but it’s a place I’ll probably remember until I die.

reviews.author: Matthew
reviews.text: If you’re in Cao Bằng, visiting God’s Eye Mountain is an absolute must. This place feels like a hidden gem straight out of a fantasy world. You can park at the top and walk down, but if you have a motorbike, I highly recommend riding—it made the experience even better! We spent two hours exploring the trails, weaving through stunning landscapes, and taking in some of the most unique scenery in Vietnam. The area has so much to offer: a small cave with water flowing through it, a waterfall, and even wild horses and buffalo roaming freely. It’s peaceful, breathtaking, and completely worth the journey. If you’re looking for an off-the-beaten-path adventure, God’s Eye Mountain should be at the top of your list!

reviews.author: Peter Elli
reviews.text: So unusual to see this mountain . Rough road in but well worth the effort. It’s a real farming area and not yet spoiled by too many tourists.. please buy a drink at the little shop onsite and relax whilst absorbing the surrounds.. I loved it .. 5 stars

reviews.author: KC
reviews.text: Its nice but not amazing. You need to walk around 5 to 10 mins if you want to be near. The coffee shop is good and owners are funny.

reviews.author: Dominik Vu
reviews.text: No tickets, no wait, just pure joy. It's stunning, we were there at noon and still it was enjoyable. Took a small boat tour for 100k per person, not the best deal but not bad. I imagine it must be even better at sunrise or sunset. The path there is tricky, either 4x4 or a bike.

reviews.author: mike
reviews.text: I had a camping stay just in front of the mountain. My tent had a perfect view and in the morning it was especially amazing to enjoy the beauty of the nature of this place. I really recommend to spend at least a night in the campsite, have an unforgettable breakfast and enjoy the moment. IMPORTANT: there is time when the lake is there and when it is dried out. In November there is no lake and it is more like a desert. So you might want to check that before you go there.

reviews.author: Giang Huynh
reviews.text: That place was so beautiful. I wished I could go down to the valley to see the lake. The slope was slipery the day I came.

reviews.author: Geraldine Gut
reviews.text: Nice view from the small Smoothie shop (Picture). Make sure to choose good weather, you cane drive down by motorbike a bit closer to the mountain. But when the ground is soaking wet due to rain the ground is quiet slippery and you need either a good bike or skills to go down there.

reviews.author: Hifdzannudin Abd Hair
reviews.text: Superb and amazing view. Recommended to all to come here, you can reach down the valley by motorcycle. You also can spend the night at their camping site. Please be aware there is no bathroom here. You can also spot small river + waterfall. You can go but the way is little challenging. Anyway, best spot for panorama view.

reviews.author: Trung Lê Quang
reviews.text: Have been this place for both dry season and rainy season. Good place for riding motorcycles around.

reviews.author: Leo van Galen
reviews.text: Such a cool natural landmark!! You have to visit this if you are near, no entry fee either!

reviews.author: Des P
reviews.text: Hell yeah - you wanna see this . Didn’t get a chance to get too close this time, but I’ll come back for a hike

reviews.author: matt
reviews.text: Excellent view, many of the motorbike tours take you up there as well as the ban gioc falls and the tiger cave

reviews.author: Leona Joyfull Life
reviews.text: God’s Eye Mountain is one of Cao Bang’s must-see natural wonders, located about 27 km northeast of the city. What makes this mountain truly unique is the large, circular hole piercing right through its lush green peak, resembling the “eye” of earth and sky. When the sun rises or sets, brilliant rays shine through the mountain’s eye, creating a breathtaking and magical spectacle. Surrounded by rolling mountain ranges, this remarkable natural feature is an ideal starting point for your adventure in Cao Bang. You can visit some nearly places: Thang Hen lake; the handmade paper village; the traditional incense village and the famous hammer and knife crafting village. No tickets needed. This is definitely a relaxing day out filled with culture and friendly people.

reviews.author: Regina L
reviews.text: November 12, not a lot of water but still nice. Great cafe at the entrance

reviews.author: Abbie
reviews.text: A lovely free experience. It’s honestly breathtaking. There is also a little cafe next to it if you want a drink whilst there

reviews.author: Lohann Kerviche
reviews.text: Beautiful scenery, if you're thinking about it. Do it
